review:
Vietnamese of this sort is basically a commodity, and Pho An Hoa does an admirable job at this staple.  It is not remarkable or especially notable, and the execution here is just fine and what you would expect.  I would like it more and definitely go if it was my neighborhood spot.  There were plenty of ethnic folks here, which is always a mark of authenticity and quality, and the food here is made with love.  But there is really nothing unique to distinguish this place from the many, and to my mind better, competitors. 

I had my regular fare: fresh rolls, followed by rare beef pho with added meatballs.  It was good, and the prices and portions were more than reasonable.  

Thank you for a lovely meal.  I would be back if I am ever in the neighborhood.
